sub_mechanisms: M_1: form algebra of attention-head projection matrices
M_2: compute equivalence classes via Murray-von Neumann (head_A ~ head_B if there exist u,v with uA=B and vB=A)
M_3: cluster heads into classes; keep one representative per class
stripped_llm_application: A head-pruning method that groups attention heads into equivalence classes via topological similarity of their projection matrices and retains one head per class.
stripping_rule_applied: removed: C*, K-theory, K_0, homotopy, operator-algebra, Murray-von-Neumann (the last term retained in M_2 as a mathematical concept name)
